breaking.title: From baby to dog: Miracle rescues giving Venezuela hope after deadly earthquakes - watch
breaking.summary: Amidst the devastation of twin earthquakes in Venezuela, remarkable rescues are offering glimmers of hope. Emergency workers and citizens are tirelessly searching for survivors beneath collapsed buildings, with a baby and a 12-year-old boy among those miraculously pulled to safety. These uplifting moments contrast sharply with the rising death toll and widespread destruction, as international aid begins to arrive.

breaking.title: Venezuela earthquake LIVE updates: Death toll climbs to 235
breaking.summary: The number of dead and injured is expected to rise with thousands reported missing after the 7.2- and 7.5-magnitude earthquakes

breaking.title: Venezuela declares state of emergency after powerful twin earthquakes; Maiquetia airport closed
breaking.summary: Buildings collapse in capital Caracas; Delcy Rodriguez says 20 aftershocks followed the earthquakes

breaking.title: Venezuela quake leaves 235 dead: Thousands feared trapped as rescue efforts intensify
breaking.summary: Northern Venezuela grapples with devastation after twin earthquakes, with the death toll climbing past 235 and thousands feared affected. Rescue efforts are underway, though residents report slow official responses. International aid is pouring in, with the US authorizing significant assistance and numerous countries dispatching rescue teams. Despite widespread destruction, the nation's vital oil infrastructure remains largely intact.

breaking.title: Couple Found Dead Inside Car In Madhya Pradesh, 3 Phones Found 50 Feet Away
breaking.summary: The bodies have been sent for post-mortem examination.

breaking.title: Ship Attack In Strait Of Hormuz Halts UN Evacuation Plan For Trapped Sailors
breaking.summary: Iran imposed a blockade of the strait during the war, sparking a global economic shock

breaking.title: Trump allies defend him to Israelis anxious over Iran deal
breaking.summary: The U.S.-Israeli relationship has been on a roller coaster, from the ​early confidence they shared after their joint attack on Iran to public disagreements between Donald Trump and Prime Minister Benjamin Netanyahu over how ⁠to end the four-month-old war

breaking.title: What we know so far
breaking.summary: The two quakes have killed at least 235 people and injured more than 4,300, with more feared trapped under rubble.
